title: Ducks vs. Golden Knights
description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for May 6 at 9:30PM ET:
If the Ducks win, the market will resolve to "Ducks".
If the Golden Knights win, the market will resolve to "Golden Knights".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vegas Golden Knights hold a 1-0 series lead over the Anaheim Ducks in the NHL Western Conference second round after a 3-1 Game 1 victory at T-Mobile Arena on May 4, sparked by Ivan Barbashev's go-ahead goal 65 seconds after Anaheim tied it in the third period. Game 2 follows tonight in Vegas, amplifying the Golden Knights' home-ice advantage and elite penalty kill (19-for-20 recently), while Ducks goaltender Lukas Dostal faces a rested Adin Hill. Vegas defenseman Jarred Lauzon is out for the series with an undisclosed injury; Anaheim's Radko Gudas remains sidelined, though the Ducks dominated the regular-season series 3-0-0, including OT wins. Momentum favors Vegas amid playoff intensity, but Ducks' resilience and Pacific Division third-place finish (43-33-6) keep it competitive.

eventMetadata.context_description: Carolina Hurricanes hold a 1-0 series lead over the Philadelphia Flyers in the Eastern Conference semifinals after a 3-2 overtime victory in Game 1 at home, showcasing their tight-checking forecheck and home-ice edge for Game 2. Hurricanes forward Nikolaj Ehlers returns from a lower-body injury that sidelined him late in their first-round sweep of Ottawa, boosting top-line scoring, though defenseman Alexander Nikishin sits out Game 2 with a concussion. Flyers winger Owen Tippett remains a game-time decision after missing Game 1 with an undisclosed injury, potentially weakening their speed on the rush; goaltender Dan Vladar starts after a solid relief effort. Both teams enter rested post-first round, but Carolina's 9-1 record in the last 10 head-to-heads underscores trader consensus on their edge amid these roster tweaks.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Vegas Golden Knights hold home-ice advantage as Pacific Division top seed hosting Game 1 Monday against the Anaheim Ducks in the Western Conference Second Round of the 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs, but traders weigh Ducks' momentum from sweeping the regular-season series 3-0 and upsetting Edmonton in Round 1 behind goaltender Lukas Dostal. Vegas relies on Carter Hart in net amid key absences like center William Karlsson (lower-body injury, postseason return unlikely short-term), while Ducks captain Radko Gudas (lower-body) skated Saturday and could return early in the best-of-7. Playoff experience favors the 2023 Cup champion Golden Knights, though Ducks' recent head-to-head dominance and offensive surge (NHL-leading 26 Round 1 goals) fuel a competitive trader consensus.

eventMetadata.context_description: Carolina Hurricanes seized a 1-0 series lead over the Philadelphia Flyers in Eastern Conference Semifinals Game 1 with a 3-0 shutout at Lenovo Center, driven by Logan Stankoven's two goals, Frederik Andersen's 28-save performance, and a perfect 4-for-4 penalty kill that neutralized Philadelphia's power play. Rested after a first-round bye, the top-seeded Hurricanes displayed depth and no rust, aided by Nikolaj Ehlers' return from a lower-body injury, while the Flyers struggled offensively without day-to-day forward Owen Tippett amid their recent grind past Pittsburgh. Game 2 on May 4 offers Carolina continued home-ice advantage; Philadelphia's bounce-back hinges on Tippett's status, goaltending response, and defensive improvements in this best-of-7.

eventMetadata.context_description: Minnesota Wild face significant hurdles in Game 1 of the Western Conference Second Round at Ball Arena, missing top-four defenseman Jonas Brodin (lower-body injury, ruled out) and with shutdown center Joel Eriksson Ek questionable after a grueling six-game series win over Dallas that exposed their penalty kill (10 power-play goals allowed). Colorado Avalanche, fresh off a dominant 4-0 sweep of Los Angeles with extra rest, leverage home-ice advantage, elite center depth led by Nathan MacKinnon, and strong goaltending from Scott Wedgewood (.950 SV% in Round 1) against Jesper Wallstedt. Recent Wild injury updates on May 2 have solidified trader consensus favoring Avalanche at 64% implied probability, reflecting their superior health, momentum, and 5-on-5 matchup edge in this heated Central Division rivalry.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vegas Golden Knights enter Game 1 of the Western Conference Second Round with home-ice advantage at T-Mobile Arena after topping the Pacific Division at 39-26-17 and dispatching Utah in six games, while the Anaheim Ducks ride momentum from a 4-2 upset over Edmonton—their first series win since 2017. Vegas boasts superior playoff experience as 2023 Stanley Cup champions and a lopsided 27-8-3 all-time head-to-head record against Anaheim, though the Ducks swept their three regular-season meetings in tight contests. Ducks captain Radko Gudas remains day-to-day with a lower-body injury after skating separately Saturday, potentially weakening their blue line; no major Golden Knights injuries reported. Both squads enjoyed similar rest, setting up a clash of Vegas' depth versus Anaheim's surging offense that led the playoffs with 26 Round 1 goals.

eventMetadata.context_description: Buffalo Sabres host the Montreal Canadiens in Game 1 of the Eastern Conference second-round Stanley Cup Playoffs series, with the higher-seeded Sabres holding home-ice advantage after clinching the Atlantic Division at 50-23-9 and defeating the Boston Bruins in the first round—their first series win since 2007. Montreal advanced via a dramatic 2-1 Game 7 victory over the Tampa Bay Lightning, fueled by goaltender Jakub Dobes' stellar .959 save percentage over the final two games and 28-of-29 stops in the clincher. Sabres forward Sam Carrick nears return after a five-week injury absence, prompting potential lineup tweaks per recent practice reports, while recent regular-season head-to-heads favored Montreal. Key factors include Buffalo's rest edge, youthful firepower from Tage Thompson and Alex Tuch, and Montreal's momentum amid high-stakes rivalry intensity.

eventMetadata.context_description: Buffalo Sabres enter Game 1 of the Eastern Conference Second Round with home-ice advantage over the Montreal Canadiens on May 6 at KeyBank Center, following a 50-23-9 regular season that topped Montreal's 48-24-10 mark in the Atlantic Division. Sabres advanced past Boston Bruins in six games, powered by Alex Lyon's dominant relief goaltending (3-1, 1.14 GAA, .955 SV% in Round 1) and scoring from Tage Thompson (seven points) and Alex Tuch (seven points). Canadiens grinded out a seven-game upset over Tampa Bay Lightning, led by Nick Suzuki's playmaking. Buffalo misses forwards Noah Ostlund (lower-body) and Sam Carrick (upper-body) through Round 2, while Montreal reports no major injuries; regular-season head-to-head split 2-2 underscores a competitive playoff clash with rest edges and depth key.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Buffalo Sabres hold home-ice advantage as the higher seed hosting Game 1 on May 6 against the Montreal Canadiens in the Eastern Conference Second Round of the 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs, following their six-game upset over the Bruins—their first advancement since 2007—and Montreal's thrilling Game 7 road win over Tampa Bay. Sabres forward injuries loom large, with centers Sam Carrick (arm) out for the season and Noah Ostlund (lower body) sidelined for the series, prompting Josh Norris to step in, while defenseman Logan Stanley remains day-to-day. Canadiens rookie goaltender Jakub Dobes impressed in net during round one, backed by Nick Suzuki's playoff-best performance, setting up a high-speed clash between youth-driven squads with evenly matched regular-season head-to-heads.
